{
  "number": 1423,
  "title": "fix(auth): refresh token rotation off-by-one on clock skew",
  "state": "OPEN",
  "body": "## Summary\nWhen the auth service clock is >30s behind the client, refresh tokens were being rejected as stale *one cycle earlier* than documented. Fixes #1408.\n\n## Changes\n- Compare against `issued_at - SKEW_TOLERANCE` instead of `issued_at`\n- Add regression test with mocked clock offset\n- Document the tolerance window in SECURITY.md\n\n## Test plan\n- [x] Unit: token_refresh_test.py::test_clock_skew\n- [x] Integration: authd/tests/refresh_flow.py\n- [ ] Deploy to staging and watch auth_refresh_rejected_total",
  "author": {"login": "ttimms"},
  "commits": {"totalCount": 4},
  "additions": 87,
  "deletions": 12,
  "changedFiles": 6,
  "mergeable": "MERGEABLE",
  "reviewDecision": "REVIEW_REQUIRED"
}

{
  "number": 307,
  "title": "crash: permission engine raises on empty deny list",
  "state": "OPEN",
  "body": "## Steps to reproduce

    PermissionEngine(allow_patterns=['*'], deny_patterns=[])

raises `ValueError: deny_patterns must be non-empty` on v0.14+. Previously (v0.13.x) this was accepted.

## Expected
Empty list should be accepted; the engine already enforces a floor on dangerous shell via the hard guard.

## Observed
See traceback in logs/engine-507.log.

## Version
godspeed 0.14.2, Python 3.13.2 on macOS 14.4.",
  "author": {"login": "rick-s"},
  "comments": {"totalCount": 3},
  "labels": [{"name": "bug"}, {"name": "security"}, {"name": "good-first-issue"}]
}

{
  "number": 1419,
  "title": "feat(api): add pagination cursor to /v1/documents",
  "state": "OPEN",
  "body": "Adds opaque cursor-based pagination to /v1/documents.\n\n- Cursor is base64(JSON({last_id, sort_key})).\n- Tamper-detection via HMAC-SHA256 signed with `DOCS_PAGINATION_SECRET`.\n- `limit` clamped to [1, 200] server-side.\n\nRequires migration 0042 (no-op data).\n\nBreaking: old `offset` param still accepted but deprecated; log-warns for 60 days.",
  "author": {"login": "akira-m"},
  "commits": {"totalCount": 11},
  "additions": 340,
  "deletions": 85,
  "changedFiles": 13,
  "mergeable": "MERGEABLE",
  "reviewDecision": "CHANGES_REQUESTED"
}

{
  "number": 508,
  "title": "security: audit event not persisted on sandbox crash",
  "state": "CLOSED",
  "body": "If the sandbox subprocess segfaults while a tool call is mid-execution, the audit log writer was being dropped with the process — the SIGKILL event never hit disk. Fixed by moving the audit writer into the parent controller.",
  "comments": {"totalCount": 7},
  "closedAt": "2026-04-08T22:14:02Z"
}

{
  "number": 312,
  "title": "feature: support MCP 1.0 ResourceTemplate",
  "state": "OPEN",
  "body": "MCP 1.0 promotes ResourceTemplate to a first-class primitive. Right now we ignore the templates/list method on client handshake. We should:\n\n1. Advertise templates via our server capability object.\n2. Resolve template URIs on read_resource calls.\n3. Add a ResourceTemplate class mirroring the TS SDK naming.\n\nBlocking multiple downstream MCP clients that only speak 1.0.",
  "comments": {"totalCount": 5},
  "labels": [{"name": "enhancement"}, {"name": "mcp"}]
}
